what: R Bindings for BebeLM CPU Inference
title: R Bindings for BebeLM CPU Inference
text: Rbebelm provides experimental R bindings upstream maximecb/bebelm, pure-Rust CPU-implementation Liquid AI LFM2.5-8B-A1B inference. R package uses savvy R/Rust boundary Rsassy-style runtime backend layout portable SIMD dispatch. package designed interactive LLM use: generation streams tokens R console soon decoded, function still returns final text, token ids, stop reason, timing statistics.

title: R Bindings for BebeLM CPU Inference
text: Install R-universe: R-universe can also publish Linux binaries universe. prefer binaries Linux, use universe binary repository pattern: Source installs require Cargo/rustc GNU make. Linux, macOS, Windows, Rbebelm builds separate Rust backend libraries possible: scalar, AVX2, AVX-512 x86_64; scalar NEON arm64. dispatcher selects best installed backend supported current CPU/runtime loading model code. model weights bundled R package. Download GGUF weights upstream model source documented bebelm, pass local path bebel_model_load().
code: install.packages(   "Rbebelm",   repos = c("https://sounkou-bioinfo.r-universe.dev", "https://cloud.r-project.org") ) options(repos = c(   Rbebelm = sprintf(     "https://sounkou-bioinfo.r-universe.dev/bin/linux/noble-%s/%s/",     R.version$arch,     substr(getRversion(), 1, 3)   ),   CRAN = sprintf(     "https://cran.r-universe.dev/bin/linux/noble-%s/%s/",     R.version$arch,     substr(getRversion(), 1, 3)   ) )) install.packages("Rbebelm")

title: R Bindings for BebeLM CPU Inference
text: Set BEBELM_WEIGHTS_FILE local GGUF path, replace weights explicit file path. BebelAgent owns token transcript decode caches sharing loaded model weights. Later turns prefill newly appended tokens. Use bebel_clear(agent) reset transcript caches without reloading GGUF. ellmer-style terminal loop, call bebel_live_console(agent) bebel_live_console(model) interactive R session. can also create console directly model: Convenience helpers still available simple calls. bebel_chat() wraps single ChatML user/assistant turn: plain text completion, use bebel_generate(): Agents can also driven lower level raw text token ids: Tools can orchestrated Agent run loop. context object private R tools hooks; sent model. tool dispatched model emits BebeLM tool-call block, prompts describe available tools expected call format. event stream can consumed programmatically. example, collect answer-text deltas suppressing console output: can also pass named list event-specific handlers directly:
code: library(Rbebelm)  weights <- Sys.getenv("BEBELM_WEIGHTS_FILE") model <- bebel_model_load(weights, num_threads = 2)  # Agent-first API: one loaded model can back several conversations. agent <- bebel_agent(model, greedy = TRUE, max_gen = 48, max_think = 16)  bebel_append_user(agent, "What is the capital of France? Answer briefly.") turn1 <- bebel_assistant_turn(agent, on_event = NULL)  bebel_append_user(agent, "And Italy?") turn2 <- bebel_assistant_turn(agent, on_event = NULL)  turn1 turn2 bebel_agent_info(agent)[c("history_tokens", "processed_tokens", "kv_tokens")] chat <- bebel_agent(model, max_gen = 256, max_think = 64) bebel_live_console(chat) #> ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ #> ║  Entering BebeLM live console.                     ║ #> ║  Type /quit or /exit to return to R.               ║ #> ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ #> >>> What is BebeLM? #> <think> #> The user asks for a brief explanation of BebeLM. #> </think> #> BebeLM is a small, CPU-focused local language model runtime written in Rust. #> #> >>> Why use the R agent API? #> The R agent keeps the transcript and decode caches alive across turns while #> sharing the loaded GGUF weights. #> #> >>> /quit bebel_live_console(model, max_gen = 256, max_think = 64) # on_event defaults to bebel_console_event(): thinking and text print live. result <- bebel_chat(   model,   "In one concise sentence, what does runtime SIMD dispatch do?",   greedy = TRUE,   max_gen = 48,   max_think = 16,   on_event = bebel_console_event(),   check_interrupt = TRUE )  result raw_result <- bebel_generate(   model,   "Runtime SIMD dispatch is useful because",   greedy = TRUE,   max_gen = 24,   max_think = 16,   on_event = bebel_console_event(),   check_interrupt = TRUE ) raw_result raw_agent <- bebel_agent(model, greedy = TRUE, max_gen = 16, max_think = 0) bebel_append(raw_agent, "The capital of France is") raw_turn <- bebel_agent_generate(raw_agent, on_event = NULL)  ids <- bebel_tokenize(model, " and its airport code is", add_bos = FALSE) bebel_append_tokens(raw_agent, ids) bebel_history(raw_agent)[1:8] bebel_token_ids()[c("TOKEN_THINK", "TOKEN_TOOL_CALL_START", "TOKEN_TOOL_CALL_END")] raw_turn$text ctx <- new.env(parent = emptyenv()) ctx$thread_id <- "thread-001" ctx$log <- character()  tools <- list(   lookup_capital = bebel_tool(     "lookup_capital",     function(args, context, call) {       context$log <- c(context$log, paste("tool", call$name, args$country))       c(France = "Paris", Italy = "Rome")[[args$country]]     },     description = "Return a capital city for a country."   ) )  hooks <- list(   tool_request = function(call, context, ...) {     context$log <- c(context$log, paste("request", call$name))   },   tool_result = function(call, result, context, ...) {     context$log <- c(context$log, paste("result", call$name, result))   } )  tool_prompt <- paste(   "Available tool:",   "lookup_capital({\"country\": <country>}) returns a capital city.",   "When a tool is needed, emit a BebeLM tool-call block containing only the call.",   "Question: what is the capital of Italy?" )  agent <- bebel_agent(model, max_gen = 128, max_think = 16) bebel_append_user(agent, tool_prompt) run <- bebel_agent_run(agent, tools = tools, context = ctx, hooks = hooks) run ctx$log deltas <- character() invisible(bebel_generate(   model,   "A text delta callback can",   greedy = TRUE,   max_gen = 12,   max_think = 16,   on_event = bebel_event_handler(     text_delta = function(event) deltas <<- c(deltas, event$delta)   ) )) paste0(deltas, collapse = "") counts <- c(text_delta = 0L, thinking_delta = 0L, done = 0L) invisible(bebel_generate(   model,   "An event handler list can",   greedy = TRUE,   max_gen = 4,   max_think = 16,   on_event = list(     text_delta = function(event) counts["text_delta"] <<- counts[["text_delta"]] + 1L,     thinking_delta = function(event) counts["thinking_delta"] <<- counts[["thinking_delta"]] + 1L,     done = function(event) counts["done"] <<- counts[["done"]] + 1L   ) )) counts

title: R Bindings for BebeLM CPU Inference
text: Generation checks R_CheckUserInterrupt() prompt prefill every decoded token, wrapped savvy’s unwind protection Ctrl-C longjmp Rust frames. Streaming event-based uses finite event protocol; bebel_event_types() reports event enum build. current enum returned bebel_event_types() includes stream lifecycle, thinking blocks, answer text blocks, tool-list blocks, tool-call blocks, done. Delta events contain delta, id, index; control start/end events include delimiter token id marker; end events contain accumulated content. Console printing just default event handler. Use on_event = NULL silent batch-style generation.

title: R Bindings for BebeLM CPU Inference
text: Rbebelm builds real Rust/savvy backend webR static wasm_simd128 backend. wasm build uses patched local copy upstream BebeLM avoids native-mmap Rayon imports Emscripten: GGUF files read webR filesystem memory matmul runs serially. mount download GGUF webR virtual filesystem, bebel_model_load() attempt load . large models can still exhaust browser/webR memory.

title: R Bindings for BebeLM CPU Inference
text: Rbebelm installs one small R shared library plus separate Rust backend libraries. R shared library owns registration dispatch; model code lives selected backend library. dispatcher checks CPU/OS support loading SIMD backends, portable binary can avoid executing unsupported instructions. Backend selection happens per R process. need benchmark debug specific backend, call rbebelm_set_backend() first native Rbebelm call fresh Rscript process: Inspect current CPU/runtime selected backend:

title: R Bindings for BebeLM CPU Inference
text: Common development commands repository root:
code: make rd           # regenerate savvy wrappers, dispatch init, NAMESPACE, and man/*.Rd make rdm          # regenerate README.md from evaluated README.Rmd make dev-install  # install the package locally from source make test         # run tinytest tests make check        # build and run R CMD check --no-manual make site         # build the pkgdown site make clean        # remove generated build artifacts
